title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During an investigation into a murder, Cladwell (also referred to as Claddock or Craddock) and Bacon discuss the timeline of a suspect who left the Balearic Islands on the 21st. Harold Crickenshaw is questioned but provides no useful information, expressing fear regarding newspaper reporters. Cladwell then questions Alfred, who claims to be living on social security while developing a new type of phonograph. Cladwell observes that Alfred’s suit appears expensive but is actually cheap. Alfred suggests that if the victim was from London, the killer must know that the Long Warehouse is a suitable location for secret murders.

Brian Eastley, the widower of Edith Crakenchurch, joins the inquiry. He speculates on whether the murder was driven by a "perversion" similar to Caligula and discusses the Crickenshaw family. He notes that Harold is married to a woman of noble descent, while Alfred is involved in "black deals." Brian also mentions that Sedgwick attracts many women and had several lovers on Ibiza.

Cladwell subsequently interviews Emma Cripsen, noting her calm demeanor and suspecting that the mystery of the stone coffin may be linked to her. He questions her regarding the victim's identity and why she previously assumed the woman was French. Emma denies having any specific reason for the assumption. When Bacon presents a small porcelain powder box, Emma claims she does not recognize it and cannot guess its owner. After the interview, Cladwell observes that Emma walks away quickly, appearing relieved.
